steps.prompt: Which word is a relative pronoun?

steps.reveal: qui = who (nom. masc. sg., subject of 'petit')

hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'quo' — 'qui' is nominative, subject of 'petit'

hints.to be small: English 'petit/petite' meaning 'small' is French — Latin 'petere' means 'to seek'
hints.to attack: While 'petere' can mean 'to attack', in this context it means 'to ask/seek'

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'petat' — 'petit' is present indicative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'petivit' — 'petit' is present indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'petet' — 'petit' is present indicative

hints.pono, ponere, posui, positum: These are the parts of 'ponere' (to place)
hints.posco, poscere, poposci, —: These are the parts of 'poscere' (to demand)
hints.repeto, repetere, repetivi, repetitum: These are the parts of 'repetere' (to seek again)

hints.to seize: That would be 'capere/rapere' — 'accipit' comes from 'accipere' (to receive)
hints.to send: That would be 'mittere' — 'accipit' comes from 'accipere' (to receive)
hints.to begin: That would be 'incipere' — 'accipit' comes from 'accipere' (to receive)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'accipiat' — 'accipit' is present indicative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'accepit' — 'accipit' is present indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'accipiet' — 'accipit' is present indicative

hints.capio, capere, cepi, captum: These are the parts of 'capere' (to seize) — 'accipere' is its compound
hints.incipio, incipere, incepi, inceptum: These are the parts of 'incipere' (to begin)
hints.recipio, recipere, recepi, receptum: These are the parts of 'recipere' (to take back)

hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'omnis' as well, but context (subject of 'accipit') shows nominative

hints.1st/2nd declension adjective: 1st/2nd decl. adj. would be 'bonus, -a, -um' — 'omnis, -e' is 3rd declension
hints.4th declension: Adjectives do not follow 4th declension — 'omnis' is 3rd declension
hints.indeclinable: 'Omnis' declines: omnis, omnis, omni, omnem, omni — it is 3rd declension
